fix(fixtures): center G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons STEP shapes at origin (#96)

* fix(fixtures): center G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons STEP shapes at origin

DRAWEXE primitive commands (box, pcylinder, pcone) place one corner or
the bottom face at the OCCT origin, but the corresponding Geant4 solids
(G4Box, G4Tubs, G4Cons) are centered at the origin. This mismatch caused
~2038/2048 DistanceToOut mismatches for G4Box in the benchmark comparison.

Fix each fixture's generate.tcl to center the shape:
- G4Box:  box seed_box -10 -15 -20 20 30 40  (corner at half-extents)
- G4Tubs: translate seed_tubs 0 0 -17.5       (shift by -height/2)
- G4Cons: translate seed_cons 0 0 -12         (shift by -height/2)

Regenerate shape.step for all three fixtures and update draw_command in
provenance.yaml to match the new generation commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add ADR 012 background service architecture

I created ADR 012 documenting the decision to implement a background
service architecture for Naked Time. The ADR covers the motivation
for moving from a stateless CLI to a CLI/service split, the six key
decisions (single-binary service mode, user-scoped process, CLI/service
separation, IPC transport selection, lifecycle management, and child
process framework), alternatives considered, an architecture diagram,
and the consequences of the decision.

I also updated docs/adrs/README.md to add ADR 012 to the index.

commit.message:
Disable kitty keyboard `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` flag (#264)

The kitty keyboard enhancement flag `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` causes
terminals to emit separate press/release/repeat events. This removes
that flag so only key press events are received.

### Changes

- **`PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags`**: removed `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` from
the flags bitmask
- **Event loop**: simplified key handler — dropped the `match key.kind`
dispatch since release events no longer arrive
- **`on_keyrelease`**: removed the method (was only used to toggle mouse
capture on Alt release in Simple mode)
- **Stale comment**: removed reference to `on_keyrelease` in the Alt
press handler

```rust
// Before
crossterm::event::PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags(
    KeyboardEnhancementFlags::DISAMBIGUATE_ESCAPE_CODES
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_EVENT_TYPES   // <-- removed
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALL_KEYS_AS_ESCAPE_CODES
)
```

The bench_navigator.cc output was restructured from a single-line-per-fixture
ray-only format to a multi-method per-fixture block format. Update the parser
and renderer in generate_bench_report.py to match:

- Section header: '=== Fixture Navigation Benchmark Results ===' (was 'Ray')
- Count line: 'Rays: N  |  Inside points: N  |  Safety points: N' (was 'Rays per fixture: N')
- Per-fixture: multi-line blocks with 6 method rows each

The renderer now shows a per-method aggregate table and a per-fixture table
with DistanceToIn/Out timing and total mismatches across all methods.
Add _parse_timing_value / _format_timing helpers to reduce duplication.

Cell format: "native → imported (ratio) [✅]" or "[N ⚠️]" for mismatches.

Implement IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and multi-head attention (#180)

* Add IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and spatial attention pooling

New visual encoder architecture for full-screen RGB observation:

Architecture:
- ResBlock: Pre-activation residual block (ReLU→Conv→ReLU→Conv + skip)
- ImpalaResNet: 3 residual stacks (5→16→32→32) with CoordConv buffers
  and spatial self-attention pooling, producing a 256-dim feature vector
- SpatialAttentionPool: 1×1 conv Q/K/V projections, scaled dot-product
  attention over all spatial positions, weighted-sum pooling
- ImpalaCombinedExtractor: ImpalaResNet + entity attention + info = 335-dim
- ImpalaSharedAgent / ImpalaMultiHeadAgent: New Network subclasses with
  forward_with_attention() for visualization

All spatial dimensions are dynamic (works for both 168×240 cropped and
176×256 full overscan modes). CoordConv channels registered as buffers.

Observation:
- New obs_kind='full-rgb': HUD trimmed, RGB (no grayscale), no viewport
  crop, frame_stack=1. Output shape: (3, H, W) normalized [0,1].

Config:
- New model-kinds in triforce.yaml: impala-shared, impala-multihead

Debugger:
- ATTENTION overlay flag in OverlayFlags enum
- Attention heatmap checkbox in View menu
- JET colormap heatmap overlay on gameplay area
- Attention weights fetched from model via forward_with_attention()

Tests:
- 28 new tests covering ResBlock, SpatialAttentionPool, ImpalaResNet,
  ImpalaSharedAgent, ImpalaMultiHeadAgent, registration, save/load,
  both screen sizes, CoordConv buffer properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-detect obs_kind from saved models in evaluate/diagnose/debug

Add infer_obs_kind() to observation_wrapper.py that determines the
observation kind (viewport/full-rgb/gameplay) and frame_stack from
the saved observation space shape. This removes the need for explicit
--obs-kind parameters when loading existing models.

- evaluate.py: uses infer_obs_kind(network.observation_space)
- diagnose.py: uses infer_obs_kind(metadata['obs_space']) at all 3 sites
- environment_bridge.py: infers from model metadata, returns obs_space
- train.py: infers from --load model when available, auto-detects from
  model_kind name for new training (impala-* defaults to full-rgb)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix IMPALA OOM: auto-increase minibatches for spatial attention

The 630-token spatial attention matrix (630×630 × batch) causes OOM
with the default 4 minibatches (minibatch_size=1024). Add
recommended_minibatches=16 to IMPALA agents, which PPO respects
unless explicitly overridden. This reduces minibatch size to 256,
cutting attention memory from ~1.9GB to ~0.5GB per minibatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add attention dropout to prevent attention collapse

SpatialAttentionPool now applies dropout (p=0.1) to the attention
weights during training. This prevents the model from fixating on a
single spatial position (e.g., the CoordConv corner) and encourages
it to learn content-dependent attention patterns.

Note: this changes the model architecture, so existing checkpoints
are not compatible — training must restart from scratch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log attention entropy and top-1 weight to tensorboard

Adds 'attention/entropy' and 'attention/top1_weight' metrics for
IMPALA models. Entropy near ln(N) means uniform attention (healthy),
near 0 means collapsed to one position (bad). Top-1 weight directly
shows the fraction of attention on the most-attended position.

commit.message:
bench/tests: skip NIST CTC navigation while assessing slowdown and G4Exceptions (#93)

Commit 3f59ca6 (PR #87) made CompareFixtureRays/Inside/Safety call
BuildNativeSolidForRequest, which for G4OCCTSolid fixtures creates a
second G4OCCTSolid from the same STEP file instead of skipping.
bench_navigator therefore now runs 2048-ray navigation over all 11 NIST
CTC AP203 compound-solid fixtures.  These are large, complex assemblies
(nist-ctc-02: 24 k STEP lines; nist-ctc-07: 7.8 k lines) whose
BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier and IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector calls are
very slow and trigger G4Exceptions from degenerate or near-surface
topology, pushing the build-test-benchmark CI job from ~10 min to 5+
hours and causing test_geometry_validation/nist-ctc-02-v1 and
nist-ctc-07-v1 to hit the CTest timeout.

Note that test_geometry_validation.cc already has an
IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture guard that correctly skips ray and
safety navigation for G4OCCTSolid fixtures; bench_navigator.cc lacked
an equivalent guard.

Changes:
- bench_navigator.cc: add IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture helper and
  guard the CompareFixtureRays/Inside/Safety block with it.  Geometry
  import and volume coverage remain via test_geometry_validation
  (geometry checks) and test_nist_ctc_inside_volume.
- CMakeLists.txt: disable all 11 test_geometry_validation/nist-ctc-*
  sub-tests until per-fixture timeouts are in place.

Both changes are explicitly temporary; they will be re-enabled once the
OCCT navigation performance on AP203 compound solids is understood.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement tilde expansion (#260)

Tilde expansion was essentially unimplemented: `~<TAB>` fell through to
`FilenameExpansion` and tried to glob `~*` in the current directory (no
results), and the `TildeExpansion` completion path was hard-disabled
with `false &&`.

## Changes

- **`tab_completion_context.rs`**: Remove `false &&` guard — `~username`
(no `/`) now routes to `TildeExpansion` instead of `FilenameExpansion`

- **`tab_completion.rs`** — rewrite `tab_complete_tilde_expansion`:
  - `~<TAB>` → returns `~/`
- `~partial<TAB>` → lists `/home/` entries + checks `/root`, returns
`~username/` (previously returned raw `/home/username/` paths)

- **`mod.rs`**: Add tilde tooltip in `wordinfo_fn` — hovering over a
tilde word (e.g. `~/docs`) shows `~/docs=/home/user/docs`, consistent
with the existing `$VAR=value` env var tooltip

Note: `~/path<TAB>` (contains `/`) was already handled correctly via
`FilenameExpansion` → `PathPatternExpansion` →
`bash_funcs::expand_filename`.

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve WCAG 2.1 AA color-contrast violations across all public pages

- Darken --text-muted CSS variable from #9A8A80 (3.27:1) to #7A6A60 (5.09:1)
- Add bg-[var(--btn-primary)] solid fallback to all gradient buttons so
  axe-core can compute white text contrast (gradients are invisible to axe)

  that previously used low-contrast --accent-rose / --accent-warm colors

  values (#EEF2E9/#FCECE7) and use dark accessible text (#366132/#7A2020)

  pre-computed #EEF2E9 so axe resolves the background correctly

- Add @axe-core/playwright dependency and accessibility.spec.ts Playwright test
